# SceneSeed

> SceneSeed is a free web app for improv teams and theaters to collect audience suggestions through a show-specific QR code. Hosts create a show, set a submission window, and share the QR with the audience. Submissions are server-time-locked to that window. Performers view, favorite, filter, and mark suggestions as used from a live dashboard.

## What it does
- Admins create improv groups and shows
- Each show has a configurable submission window (open/close times) and a public audience URL like `/s/?c=coral-2042`
- The window is enforced server-side via Firestore security rules — not just on the frontend
- Audiences scan a QR code and submit short text suggestions during the window
- Duplicate suggestions are rejected automatically (deterministic doc IDs)
- Performers see suggestions live, can favorite, hide, mark as used, search, and filter
- A separate ensemble share link with expiry lets other team members view suggestions read-only

## Stack
- Vanilla HTML / CSS / JavaScript (no framework, no build step)
- Firebase Auth (email link passwordless + Google OAuth)
- Firebase Firestore (security-rules-based time-window enforcement)
- Hosted on GitHub Pages
- Google Analytics 4

## Pages
- [/](/) — landing page, what SceneSeed is, sign in
- [/privacy.html](/privacy.html) — privacy policy
- /s/?c={publicCode} — audience submission page (no login)
- /view/?t={shareToken} — ensemble read-only view (no login, expires after show)
- /dashboard/ — admin dashboard (login required)

## Public code format
Show codes follow `{color}-2###` — for example `coral-2042` or `teal-2007`. The "2" prefix is a decade marker for 2026–2029. Shorter color names are used first, longer color names later in the year-tier.

## Repo
Source: https://github.com/itsavibecode/sceneseed (MIT license)
